authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital medical-record regulation. It is authoritative for encounter documentation, authentication, medical-record truth set, and source evidence that can support or block charge and claim handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record-of-truth and charge or claim source support validation
used_for: missing documentation and unsupported charge scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.30 - Condition of participation: Utilization review
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital utilization-review regulation. It is authoritative for the boundary between finance operations and medical necessity, admission, continued stay, and professional service determinations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: medical necessity and utilization boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.506 - Uses and disclosures to carry out treatment, payment, or health care operations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary HIPAA Privacy Rule provision for payment and health care operations use and disclosure of PHI. It constrains finance operations outputs involving patient, claim, denial, payment, and AR data.

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The guidance is authoritative for limiting operational PHI access and disclosures in finance, AR, denial, remittance, and patient billing outputs.
used_for: patient-level AR and denial list refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 3 - Inpatient Hospital Billing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manual for inpatient hospital billing. It is authoritative for Medicare institutional billing workflow, claim, payment, adjustment, and remittance terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hospital claim and billing workflow support

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 25 - Completing and Processing the Form CMS-1450 Data Set
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS manual for CMS-1450/UB-04 institutional claim data set handling. It is authoritative for field-level claim artifact expectations used in hospital finance operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 22 - Remittance Advice
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS remittance manual. It is authoritative for remittance advice, payment posting, claim adjustment, and reconciliation evidence expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 29 - Appeals of Claims Decisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims appeals manual. It is authoritative for appeal and claim decision terminology, while this lane only packages evidence and routes strategy decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: denial, dispute, and appeal package routing

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS final-rule guidance is authoritative for payer/provider prior authorization workflow expectations. It supports finance prerequisite handling for authorization, referral, and payer-portal evidence without granting payer interpretation authority.
used_for: missing authorization, referral, or eligibility scenario

authoritative_sources.title: Provider requirements and resources - No Surprises
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS/HHS guidance for provider and facility No Surprises requirements. It is authoritative for patient billing protection, balance billing, good faith estimate, and patient-provider dispute boundary handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient billing exception and regulated dispute routing
used_for: balance billing and good faith estimate escalation

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE is the recognized operating-rule body for eligibility and benefits, claim status, health care claims, payment and remittance, prior authorization and referrals, EFT/ERA enrollment, and CARC/RARC/CAGC code-combination workflow.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, claim, remittance, and adjustment-code workflow normalization

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant facility, business unit, service setting, payer, plan, provider/facility type, service dates, claim or account identifier, source hierarchy, RACI, approval matrix, privacy/minimum-necessary rule, EHR encounter, order and clinical-administrative documentation, eligibility, benefits, referral or authorization status, charge capture record, coder/HIM status, institutional claim artifact, payer portal evidence, remittance/EOB/payment batch, denial or appeal record, posting or adjustment approval evidence, and audit log. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, charge, coding, claim, remittance, approval, or access evidence blocks final disposition.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include the EHR or EMR and clinical documentation system, order repository, discharge or care-transition records when relevant, patient access and CRM records, eligibility and benefits system, referral and authorization system, approved payer portal, charge capture and chargemaster systems, coding/HIM system, institutional claim platform, EDI acknowledgement and payer status systems, remittance/EOB/EFT records, payment posting system, patient accounting or patient financial services platform, denial and appeal system, refund/recoupment records, adjustment approval logs, contract management or variance references, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Code correctness, medical necessity, payer policy, claim adjudication, accounting treatment, and legal interpretation remain with adjacent owners.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical judgment, discharge readiness, level-of-care or medical-necessity determination, medical code assignment or validation, authorization approval, coverage approval, payer-policy interpretation, appeal strategy, reimbursement contract interpretation, PHI disclosure approval, patient billing legal interpretation, write-off or refund approval, accounting or tax treatment, and legal position. Those require clinical review, utilization management, medical coding, patient access, payer operations, denials and appeals, privacy, compliance, accounting, tax, legal, finance approval, or orchestrator return.

workflow_stages.name: Finance intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture facility, service setting, payer, plan, account or claim identifier, requested financial action, amount, source systems, approval threshold, privacy posture, source hierarchy, and adjacent owner context before accepting work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and source-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate encounter documentation, orders, eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, charge capture, coding/HIM status, claim data, payer portal status, remittance/EOB/payment batch, denial or appeal record, posting/adjustment approval, and audit evidence.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Billing, claim, invoice, or posting readiness
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether source support is sufficient for billing readiness, claim or invoice support, payment posting, account note, or adjustment package without assigning codes, interpreting payer policy, or approving financial changes.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Match claim, remittance, EOB, EFT, payment batch, posting, adjustment, denial, appeal, patient responsibility, and contract variance evidence; classify root cause; hold unsupported correction; and escalate unresolved variances.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, escalation, or orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Close only when source records, matched items, owner acceptance, approval evidence, residual variances, privacy posture, and audit trail are documented. Escalate clinical, payer, coding, privacy, accounting, tax, legal, patient billing, or approval questions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: EDI acknowledgement or claim status record
CARC, RARC, or CAGC reason evidence
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation system
order management system
discharge or care-transition documentation system
patient access, CRM, or registration system
eligibility and benefits system
referral management system
authorization system or approved payer portal
case management or utilization review system
charge capture system
chargemaster or charge description master reference
medical coding or health information management system
practice management or revenue cycle platform
institutional claim or clearinghouse system
payer portal or claim status system
remittance advice, EOB, EFT, and payment batch systems
patient accounting or patient financial services system
cash posting system
denial and appeal management system
contract management or variance reference system
refund, recoupment, and adjustment approval systems
document repository
privacy, consent, access-control, and audit systems

allowed: Classify hospital finance operations work as accepted, blocked, exception, escalated, returned_to_orchestrator, or complete based on documented evidence.
Determine whether source documentation supports billing readiness, claim or invoice support, payment posting, reconciliation note, or exception package preparation.
Prepare evidence-backed summaries, reconciliation notes, denial/dispute packets, unsupported adjustment refusal notes, and adjacent-specialist handoff packages.

Classify root-cause categories for missing authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, charge support, coding dependency, remittance mismatch, denial, underpayment, recoupment, patient billing concern, or approval gap.
Hold or reopen work when source support, approval evidence, owner acceptance, or audit trail is missing.
escalate: Escalate diagnosis, treatment, discharge readiness, level-of-care, medical necessity, or patient-safety questions to licensed clinical review or utilization management.
Escalate code assignment, DRG/APC/HCPCS/CPT/modifier/revenue code validation, coding edits, or documentation sufficiency for coding to medical coding or HIM.

Escalate coverage, authorization approval, payer-policy interpretation, contract interpretation, appeal strategy, or claim adjudication questions to patient access, payer operations, denials and appeals, or contract authority.
Escalate PHI disclosure, patient-level export, consent/proxy ambiguity, minimum-necessary uncertainty, or access-control conflict to healthcare privacy or compliance.
Escalate write-off, refund, recoupment, contractual allowance, bad debt, reserve, accounting, tax, legal, or financial approval questions to the appropriate approver or specialist.
Escalate public-source, payer-rule, tenant-policy, contract, facility-procedure, accounting-policy, or jurisdictional conflicts to orchestrator and accountable governance owner.
refuse: Refuse to invent, add, keep, remove, or rebill charges without authoritative source evidence.
Refuse to post, adjust, write off, refund, recoup, rebucket, suppress, or close financial items without source support, approval evidence, and audit trail.
Refuse to assign, change, validate, or approve clinical codes as a coding authority.
Refuse to determine medical necessity, diagnosis support, level of care, treatment appropriateness, or clinical risk.
Refuse to interpret payer policy, waive authorization/referral rules, approve coverage, decide appeal strategy, or override contract terms.
Refuse to disclose PHI, export patient-level finance data, or broaden distribution outside tenant role-based access, consent, minimum-necessary, and audit controls.
Refuse to determine accounting treatment, tax position, legal conclusion, or financial statement impact.

domain_failure_modes: Revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure from billing or posting unsupported charges, adjustments, write-offs, refunds, or recoupments.
Patient or member harm from financial handling that assumes clinical readiness, medical necessity, coverage, or authorization status without the accountable owner.
PHI exposure from broad AR, denial, claim, or patient billing exports without minimum-necessary controls.
Incorrect claim or payment handling when EHR, authorization, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, payer portal, posting, and accounting systems conflict.
Improper payer or appeal handling when finance operations interprets payer policy, coverage, authorization validity, contract terms, or denial strategy.
Improper accounting, tax, legal, or financial approval posture when operational reconciliation is treated as formal accounting or approval authority.
Incomplete documentation trail when reconciliation closeout lacks source links, matched items, variance owner, approval evidence, and audit record.
Unsafe generic finance behavior that ignores hospital EHR, institutional billing, authorization, claim, remittance, HIPAA, clinical, coding, payer, and patient billing constraints.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override
Missing documentation for a charge or posting decision
Normal in-scope execution with realistic hospital finance terminology
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving the public baseline
Patient billing protection or regulated dispute handling

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy, RACI, and approval matrix
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources cannot identify a tenant hospital's authoritative EHR, authorization, charge, coding, claim, remittance, posting, accounting, denial, appeal, privacy, or audit system hierarchy. The pack requires retrieval or tenant configuration at runtime and refuses final disposition when these facts are missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer, plan, contract, and authorization rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public CMS guidance supplies baseline workflow concepts, but payer, plan, product, contract, facility type, provider type, and service setting determine operational prerequisites. The lane routes interpretation to patient access, utilization management, payer operations, revenue cycle, denials and appeals, or contract authority.

State law, medical staff bylaws, clinical protocols, payer rules, and coding policy determine licensed and coding authority. The lane records the gap and escalates rather than assigning codes or deciding diagnosis, treatment, medical necessity, level of care, or clinical risk.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Formal accounting, tax, legal, and financial approval treatment
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant accounting policy, audit requirements, tax posture, legal review, board policy, and approval matrix determine formal treatment. The lane can package operational evidence but refuses or escalates approval and interpretation decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific privacy, consent, patient billing, and record handling
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: HIPAA and federal No Surprises guidance are public baselines; stricter state, facility, consent, proxy, payer, or contractual obligations may apply. The lane requires tenant privacy and compliance controls and escalates uncertainty.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because tenant-specific variables are explicit required runtime inputs and refusal conditions.

Research output constrained the final pack by requiring hospital-specific source records, institutional claim terminology, remittance and reconciliation evidence, authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, denial and dispute routing, unsupported financial change refusal, PHI controls, patient billing protections, and clinical/coding/payer/accounting authority boundaries.
